### The Naga in Khmer Temple Architecture Nowhere is the importance of the Naga more visible than in Khmer temple architecture, particularly during the Angkor period (9th–15th centuries), when monumental temples like Angkor Wat, Bayon, and Baphuon were constructed as cosmic microcosms. Naga balustrades, typically with five or seven heads, line temple causeways and stairways. These multi-headed serpents are often shown flaring dramatically at the ends, their bodies stretching like a coiling guardrail along sacred paths.  ...
